feature: Meal Swap UI fixes — width, language, instruction steps

prompts.description: Constrain MealSwapSheet width to match existing components
prompts.prompt: In components/meal-swap-sheet.tsx, the bottom sheet is too wide on desktop. Constrain the sheet content to match the max-width of the existing meal card components in daily-view.tsx. Typically this means adding a max-w-lg or max-w-xl with mx-auto to the sheet content container so it stays centered and doesn't stretch full screen width on larger viewports. Check how other components in the app handle responsive width and match that pattern. Follow CLAUDE.md guidelines.

prompts.description: Force meal names to English unless user language is Korean
prompts.prompt: The swap meal API and meal generation are returning Korean meal names even when the app language is set to English. In app/api/swap-meal/route.ts, update the Claude prompt to explicitly generate meal names, ingredients, and recipe instructions in English by default. Only use Korean if the user's language preference is set to Korean. Check how app/api/generate-meal-plan/route.ts handles the language setting and apply the same pattern to the swap endpoint. The userProfile should contain the language preference — pass it through and use it in the AI prompt. Follow CLAUDE.md guidelines.

prompts.description: Fix swapped meal instructions to show numbered steps
prompts.prompt: After swapping a meal, the recipe instructions are displayed as one single block of text under step 1 instead of being broken into separate numbered steps. In app/api/swap-meal/route.ts, update the Claude prompt to explicitly request that recipe instructions be returned as an array of individual step strings, not one long concatenated string. Then in the component that renders meal instructions (check daily-view.tsx or related components), ensure the instructions array is mapped to display each step with its own number (1, 2, 3, etc). Match the formatting pattern used by the original meal generation in generate-meal-plan/route.ts. Follow CLAUDE.md guidelines.
